rationale: Verified at 77d8a1a9. The branch-probe fix genuinely closes G2's residue: today orphans.go:95 (`if !branchExistsFn(beadBranch) continue`, branchExistsFn=gitutil.BranchExists, gitops.go:94-100 return cmd.Run()==nil) collapses probe errors to `false`, un-flagging the orphan while FinalizeEpic's worktree-listing loop (:381-422, independent of BranchExists) still merges it. The revision adds BranchExistsE as a 4th fail-closed leg in the error-preserving core; the gate REFUSES on it before any terminal mutation (placement anchored before impl.go:327 phase-reconcile / :343 epic-close / :372 FinalizeEpic), so the loop never runs. BranchExistsE sits beside an unchanged BranchExists; the fail-open wrapper preserves complete/next/doctor byte-identically; gitutil added to execution Impacted Domains. Completeness: all four legs + R3 metadata + R3 plan-enumeration fail-closed; R2 advisory the only fail-open leg, never load-bearing. Only the two disclosed residuals (raw-git-merge'd never-refuted-RC; cross-spec/global-loop blp6) remain un-gated. Truthful-recovery split (item 4) grounded on complete.go's pre-3.75 merge-base error, preserving recoverability. All 12 named tests absent; ScanOrphanedClosedBeads 0 code hits; h4n5==1; AC10 exactly-one=workflow; cmd/** dropped. Hatches keep 114's 'except the gate, not the obligation'. No residual hole or false-refusal found.

findings.item_1_branch_probe_fail_open: ADDRESSED — BranchExistsE(name)(bool,error) added beside unchanged BranchExists (gitops.go:94); the error-preserving core (ScanOrphanedClosedBeads) consumes it as a 4th fail-closed leg alongside epic-lookup/bd-list/ancestry; gate REFUSES on it before FinalizeEpic; fail-open wrapper FindOrphanedClosedBeads keeps swallowing it (complete/next/doctor byte-identical); AC1(b) adds TestApproveImpl_BranchProbeErrorFailsClosed (named, absent=RED) + TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving; internal/gitutil/** added to execution Impacted Domains/Scope. Trace confirms the gate now refuses on a probe error before the merge loop actor.
findings.item_2_ac_non_discriminating: ADDRESSED — every AC1-AC7 chains grep -q 'func Test<Name>' with an exact-named go test -run; all 12 named tests verified absent at 77d8a1a9 (RED); AC4 anchors on ScanOrphanedClosedBeads, which has 0 code hits repo-wide (only review JSONs + spec.md).
findings.item_3_ac7_import_cycle: ADDRESSED — AC7 split: (a) TestCompleteRun_RegatesAlreadyClosedOrphan package-local in internal/complete; (b) TestApproveImpl_PassesAfterOrphanSettled in internal/approve. Grounding confirmed: no actual approve<->complete import edge today (only comments), so the split avoids the complete->approve->complete cycle the R1 form would create.
findings.item_4_branchless_recovery_untruthful: ADDRESSED — complete.go's step-3.5 merge-base error (exec.MergeBase before the step-3.75 reconcile) confirmed; the branch-less obligation refusal now carries a DISTINCT recourse naming the branch-ref restoration prerequisite before `mindspec complete <bead>`, separate from the with-branch message; AC6(a) asserts it; ADR-0035 touchpoint updated; branchless reconcile deferred to h4n5. Recoverability preserved (with-branch case still runs a bare complete).
findings.item_6_ac10_exactly_one: ADDRESSED — AC10 upgraded to a whole-set grep -l invariant across all .mindspec/domains/*/OWNERSHIP.yaml; verified exactly one claimant of internal/lifecycle/** = workflow (execution/core/context-system carry 0).
findings.item_7_cmd_overdeclared_and_self_ownersh: ADDRESSED — cmd/** dropped from the workflow declaration (no cmd/ change surface; next.go:459 is descriptive only); execution's missing self-ownership OWNERSHIP.yaml entry evidence-refuted as a pre-existing gap out of 115's scope.
